title: 2026 01 05 Rankett Partner Program
html:
TL;DR

  • Pre-meetup formalization of the Rankett white-label partner program: $998/seat to Rankett, $3K–$6K to client, $2K–$5K margin to agency, no upfront cost — the same math the Jan 7 meetup prep doc later compresses into FAQ form.
  • Introduces the Partner Activation Workflow gate: agency profile → 5-min training video → quiz pass → payment method on file before the "Add Client" button unlocks. This is the operational version of the 60 day activation rule — no quiz, no billable clients.
  • Locks in the no work without activation + no refunds for completed periods + no custom builds hardline rules; positions Rankett as a white label product, not a dev shop.
  • Documents 5 distinct offer templates (60-Day Sprint, Margin Multiplier, Performance Partnership, Quick Win+Upgrade, 3-Year Lock) with offer-selection playbook by agency type — the artillery the Diddles+Yahir pitch team brings to discovery calls.
  • Surfaces the live operational fear underneath the sales sheen: "too many say yes too fast and you break" — the real risk is fulfillment overload from undisciplined partner intake, not lack of demand.

Core content

The doc is the GTM bible's infrastructure layer: what Rankett delivers, what the agency owns, and the mechanical rules that keep the pooled data network effect from being polluted by tire-kickers. The selectivity ("5 agencies/month, no exceptions") is positioned as quality protection but functionally is also fulfillment-load throttling — Yahir is sole-operating with Diddles and can't fulfill 50 agencies onboarding 100 clients in Q1.

The 5 offers map to a sales taxonomy: skeptical → Performance Partnership; ready-to-commit → 3-Year Lock; testing → Quick Win+Upgrade. Each carries an AI visibility score guarantee with measurable % thresholds tied to the Rankett dashboard — never lead-counts, never revenue (because "too many variables, can't isolate AI search").

The Onboarding Portal spec — branded 5-min client quiz, parsed checklist, partner-approves-before-fulfillment-starts — is the rule that protects the "5-minute onboarding" sales promise. Combined with the No Intake, No Work clause, it creates a hard interlock against the partner-blames-Rankett-for-slow-results failure mode.

Stub-promotion notes

  • 60 day activation rule reaches 3+ mentions across summaries (this + 2026 01 07 rankett meetup prep guide + rankett) — already promoted in earlier slice; re-cited here.
  • whitelabel strategy, pooled data network effect, ai visibility engine all referenced — existing pages.
